content: ICM curiosity mechanisms can be re-purposed to actively compress redundant information in an LLM agent's working memory, dynamically expanding effective reasoning capacity by prioritizing novel, task-critical state representations over redundant memorized context. This reframes working memory limits not as a fixed architectural constraint, but as a suboptimal allocation of memory capacity to predictable, low-value information that curiosity can naturally prune.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
